summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones the matching upstream D-Tale commit, stores an HTML payload in an attacker-controlled dataset, enables HTML rendering for that column, opens the page in headless Chromium, and verifies same-origin exfiltration of a secret from a separate victim dataset.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause passive page load of the attacker dataset to execute injected JavaScript and exfiltrate the victim dataset secret VICTIM_TOP_SECRET from another D-Tale instance on the same origin.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the reproduction image, installed the pinned frontend dependencies, compiled the target frontend bundles, started D-Tale, created attacker and victim datasets, visited /dtale/main/2 in Playwright, and printed verifier JSON showing {"status": "ok", "attacker_data_id": "2", "secret": "VICTIM_TOP_SECRET", "exfiltrated": "VICTIM_TOP_SECRET"}.
verification_method: The verifier first confirms that the payload round-trips through /dtale/data/<attacker_data_id>, then launches headless Chromium to load the attacker page and waits for a local exfiltration listener to receive the secret fetched through same-origin calls to /dtale/processes and /dtale/data/<victim_data_id>.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run successfully created an attacker-controlled dataset and updated its column format to {"fmt":{"html":true}}.
The browser visit to /dtale/main/2 triggered same-origin requests to /dtale/processes and /dtale/data/1?ids=["0-0"].
The verifier observed and printed the success JSON {"status": "ok", "attacker_data_id": "2", "secret": "VICTIM_TOP_SECRET", "exfiltrated": "VICTIM_TOP_SECRET"}.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
